title: leucanthemum sylvaticum subsp. sylvaticum
intro: Leucanthemum sylvaticum subsp. sylvaticum, also known as the woodland daisy, is a perennial plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the temperate regions of Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of Leucanthemum sylvaticum subsp. sylvaticum include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Leucanthemum sylvaticum subsp. sylvaticum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, making it a versatile and valuable addition to traditional medicine.
